format_discipline.notes: First non-empty line is '# Dictionaries' (no YAML frontmatter). reading.meta.json exists, parses, and carries word_count, estimated_read_minutes, citations_used, sections. Exactly six H2 sections in required order: Overview, Key Concepts, Worked Example, In Practice, Key Takeaways, References.

length.notes: Declared word_count 1985 falls within [1800,2200] (PASS). However 1985/230 = 8.63 min, below the [9,11] floor; sidecar's estimated_read_minutes=10 is also inconsistent with its own word count. Metadata inconsistency, non-blocking since the substantive word-count gate passes. Same structural note as 5.1: the word and minute ranges only jointly satisfy at ~2070-2200 words.

citations.notes: Primary [1] realpython.com/python-dicts cited many times across Key Concepts. [2] dictionary-comprehension and [3] W3Schools nested both appear inline and in References. No orphan markers or orphan bibliography entries.

forward_references.notes: None of the 5.3 forward-reference terms appear by name: no 'Counter', no 'defaultdict', no 'generator', no 'iter'/iterator, no 'yield' keyword. 'yields keys' (line 74) is ordinary English ('iterating a dict yields keys'), not the yield keyword or a generator reference. The dedicated counting/grouping tool is referred to only obliquely as 'a dedicated tool ... later this week' (lines 98, 141) without naming it — compliant with the allowed vague phrasing. Only prior topics (P1, P2, 4.1 Lists, 4.2 Tuples, 5.1 Sets) and this corpus are referenced.

faithfulness.notes: Sampled and traced to corpus: (1) dict = mutable mapping of unique keys to values, corpus 5.1; (2) keys hashable/immutable, list invalid key, corpus 5.1; (3) three creation forms incl dict() kwargs become strings, corpus 5.2; (4) bracket access raises KeyError, in tests keys not values, corpus 5.2; (5) d[k]=v both add and overwrite, del and pop() with optional default, corpus 5.3; (6) keys()/values()/items(), for k in d == d.keys(), items() returns view objects, corpus 5.4; (7) sorted(d) sorts keys returns new list, sorted(d.items(), key=lambda kv: kv[1]) + reverse=True, corpus 5.5; (8) get() reads without modifying, setdefault() inserts-and-returns, setdefault(k,[]).append pattern, corpus 5.6; (9) nested dict, chained brackets, get() one level down, JSON shape, corpus 5.7; (10) dict comprehension key:value colon, if filter, inversion, uniqueness caveat, corpus 5.8; (11) word-count get(w,0)+1 then rank by value, corpus 6; (12) A2 portfolio framing, corpus 4/7. Zero unsupported (threshold >2 to fail).

tone_fit.notes: Concrete, plainspoken, beginner voice throughout, with runnable examples, inline output comments, and do/don't rules of thumb. No academic hedging phrases ('generally considered', 'many experts believe', etc.). Some sentences exceed 25 words but the overall register is plain and direct.

coverage.notes: All 7 corpus sub-topics present: (1) create/access + KeyError (lines 24-41); (2) CRUD add/modify/delete via assignment, del, pop (lines 43-57); (3) looping keys/values/items (lines 59-70); (4) sorting by key & by value (lines 72-85); (5) safe access get/setdefault (lines 87-98); (6) nested dictionaries (lines 100-110); (7) comprehension + inversion (lines 112-123). No sub-topic dropped.

findings.detail: Read-time inconsistency: declared word_count 1985 -> 8.63 min at 230 wpm, below the [9,11] target floor, while sidecar declares estimated_read_minutes=10. Non-blocking (word count passes [1800,2200]). To satisfy both gates jointly, add ~90-215 words of prose (e.g., expand Worked Example or In Practice) or correct estimated_read_minutes to ~9.

verdict: PASS. Format, length (word count), citations, no-forward-reference (Counter/defaultdict/generators/iter absent by name), faithfulness (0 unsupported), tone, and full 7/7 sub-topic coverage all clean. Sole non-blocking note: computed read-time 8.63 min sits below the [9,11] floor while declared minutes=10.
